bio: Heydar Moslehi served as Iran's Minister of Intelligence during [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s second term (2009–2013). His appointment became the epicentre of the Ahmadinejad–Khamenei power struggle: Ahmadinejad attempted to dismiss Moslehi in 2011, but [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) publicly overruled the dismissal — prompting Ahmadinejad to boycott cabinet meetings for eleven days. This was the most visible public rupture in the Ahmadinejad–Khamenei relationship. A mid-ranking cleric, Moslehi had previously been head of the Organisation of Islamic Endowments and was an Ahmadinejad loyalist until the falling out.
editorial_notes: Born 1956. Intelligence Minister 2009–2013. His dismissal by Ahmadinejad was overruled by Khamenei in 2011, triggering the biggest constitutional standoff of Ahmadinejad's presidency.
